title: BES-27: Relax aggressive staleTime on Player Detail queries
description:
## Problem
Player Detail page has 2 queries with overly aggressive staleTime causing unnecessary refetches. Also profile loading side-effect refetches on every trade list change.

## BEFORE
- **Define:** Increase staleTime on 2 query hooks + debounce profile loading
- **Scope:** 3 files
- **Criteria:**
  - [ ] tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
  - [ ] All existing tests green
  - [ ] grep confirms new staleTime values

## Changes Required

### 1. EDIT: `src/lib/queries/trades.ts`
- Line 41: usePlayerTrades has staleTime: ONE_MIN. Change to staleTime: FIVE_MIN
- Add const FIVE_MIN = 5 * 60 * 1000; at top (after ONE_MIN and TWO_MIN on lines 8-9)

### 2. EDIT: `src/features/fantasy/queries/events.ts`
- Find useHoldingLocks function (around line 43-57)
- Change staleTime: ONE_MIN to staleTime: TWO_MIN
- Verify TWO_MIN constant exists in file, add if missing: const TWO_MIN = 2 * 60 * 1000;

### 3. EDIT: `src/components/player/detail/hooks/usePlayerDetailData.ts`
- Lines 130-146: Profile loading useEffect
- Add a 2-second debounce via setTimeout to prevent rapid refetches:
- Wrap the getProfilesByIds call in setTimeout(fn, 2000)
- Return cleanup: return () => clearTimeout(timer);
- This prevents N+1 profile fetches when trade data updates rapidly

## AFTER Checklist
- [ ] tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- [ ] vitest run -- all tests green
- [ ] grep -n staleTime src/lib/queries/trades.ts shows FIVE_MIN on usePlayerTrades
- [ ] Self-review all changed files
